data.name: Primary Efficacy Objective for Part 1: Cohort A (METex14 skipping alterations)
data.text: To assess the efficacy of tepotinib in subjects with advanced (locally advanced or metastatic) non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC), as per objective response (confirmed complete response [CR] or partial response [PR]) determined according to Response Evaluation Criteria in Solid Tumors (RECIST) Version 1.1, based on independent review in: Subjects tested positive for METex14 skipping alterations, regardless of MET amplification status; Subjects tested positive for METex14 skipping alterations based on liquid biopsy (LBx), regardless of MET amplification status; Subjects tested positive for METex14 skipping alterations based on tumor biopsy (TBx), regardless of MET amplification status.

data.name: Primary Efficacy Objective for Part 1: Cohort B (MET amplification)
data.text: To assess the efficacy of tepotinib in subjects with advanced (locally advanced or metastatic) NSCLC, as per objective response (confirmed CR or PR) determined according to RECIST Version 1.1, based on independent review in: Subjects tested positive for MET amplification in LBx, and negative for METex14 skipping alterations.

data.name: Primary Efficacy Objective for Part 2: Cohort C (confirmatory part for METex14 skipping alterations)
data.text: To assess the efficacy of tepotinib in subjects with advanced (locally advanced or metastatic) NSCLC, as per objective response (confirmed CR or PR) determined according to RECIST Version 1.1, based on independent review in: Subjects tested positive for METex14 skipping alterations, regardless of MET amplification status; Subjects tested positive for METex14 skipping alterations based on LBx, regardless of MET amplification status; Subjects tested positive for METex14 skipping alterations based on TBx, regardless of MET amplification status.

data.name: Secondary Efficacy Objective
data.text: To further assess the efficacy of tepotinib in terms of: Objective response as per Investigator; Duration of response by Independent Review Committee (IRC); Duration of response by Investigator; Objective disease control as per IRC; Objective disease control as per Investigator; Progression free survival as per IRC; Progression free survival as per Investigator; Overall survival.

data.name: Secondary Safety and Tolerability Objective
data.text: To assess the tolerability and safety of tepotinib in terms of: Number of subjects with treatment-emergent adverse events (TEAEs) and deaths; Number of subjects with markedly abnormal clinical laboratory tests; Number of subjects with markedly abnormal vital signs, electrocardiogram (ECG), and physical examination.

data.name: Secondary Pharmacokinetic Objective
data.text: To assess pharmacokinetics (PK) of tepotinib and its metabolite(s)

data.name: Secondary Health-Related Quality of Life Objective
data.text: To assess Health-Related Quality of Life.

data.name: Exploratory Biomarker Objective
data.text: To explore a possible link between biomarkers of mesenchymal-epithelial transition factor (c-Met) pathway activation, other relevant oncogenic pathways in plasma, serum and tumor tissue, and the activity of tepotinib

data.name: Exploratory QT/QTc Interval Concentration Relationship Objective
data.text: To explore the QT/QTc interval concentration relationship based on Cycle 1, Day 1 and Cycle 2, Day 1 data

data.name: Exploratory Exposure-Response Relationship Objective
data.text: To investigate the exposure-response relationship.

data.name: Exploratory Efficacy Objective for Part 1: Cohort B (GCN gain subpopulations)
data.text: To assess efficacy of tepotinib in subjects with advanced (locally advanced or metastatic) NSCLC, in: Subjects with gene copy number (GCN) gain ≥ 4 and < 6 irrespective of LBx test result; Subjects with GCN gain ≥ 6 irrespective of LBx test result.

data.name: Objective Response Rate by IRC
data.text: Objective response (confirmed CR or PR) determined according to RECIST Version 1.1, based on independent review (IRC).

data.name: Objective Response Rate by Investigator
data.text: Objective response (confirmed CR or PR) determined according to RECIST Version 1.1, as per Investigator

data.name: Duration of Response by IRC
data.text: Duration of response as per IRC

data.name: Duration of Response by Investigator
data.text: Duration of response as per Investigator

data.name: Objective Disease Control Rate by IRC
data.text: Objective disease control (DCR) as per IRC

data.name: Objective Disease Control Rate by Investigator
data.text: Objective disease control as per Investigator

data.name: Progression Free Survival by IRC
data.text: Progression free survival (PFS) as per IRC

data.name: Progression Free Survival by Investigator
data.text: Progression free survival as per Investigator

data.name: Overall Survival
data.text: Overall survival (OS).

data.name: EQ-5D-5L
data.text: EQ-5D-5L

data.name: EORTC QLQ-C30
data.text: EORTC QLQ-C30

data.name: EORTC QLQ-LC13
data.text: QLQ-LC13.

data.name: Number of Treatment-Emergent Adverse Events
data.text: Number of TEAEs based upon the Medical Dictionary for Regulatory Activities and Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events of the National Cancer Institute Version 4.03

data.name: Number of Deaths
data.text: Number of deaths

data.name: Markedly Abnormal Clinical Laboratory Tests
data.text: Number of subjects with markedly abnormal clinical laboratory tests

data.name: Markedly Abnormal Vital Signs, ECG, Physical Examination
data.text: Number of subjects with markedly abnormal vital signs, ECG, physical examination, including change in body weight and Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group Performance Status.

data.name: Pharmacokinetic Parameters (CL/f, V/f)
data.text: Plasma concentrations from sparse PK sampling to allow population PK analysis. Sparse PK sampling will be performed predose, at 1.5 hours postdose and at 4 hours postdose on Cycle 1, Day 1 and on Cycle 2, Day 1. Endpoints include, but are not limited to, CL/f and V/f derived from plasma concentrations. Population PK analysis will be reported separately. The PK of tepotinib and its metabolites will be assessed using a population PK analysis approach.

data.name: Biomarkers of c-Met Pathway Activation and Correlation with Activity
data.text: Biomarkers of c-Met pathway activation and other relevant oncogenic pathways in plasma, serum and tumor tissue and their potential correlation with the activity of tepotinib.

data.name: QT/QTc Interval Concentration Relationship
data.text: QT/QTc interval concentration relationship based on Cycle 1, Day 1 and Cycle 2, Day 1 data

data.name: Exposure-Response Relationship
data.text: Exposure-response relationship

data.name: Objective Response Rate in GCN Gain Subpopulations
data.text: Objective response (confirmed CR or PR) determined according to RECIST Version 1.1, based on independent review (IRC) in subjects with gene copy number (GCN) gain ≥ 4 and < 6 or GCN gain ≥ 6.

data.name: Primary Estimand: ORR by IRC in Part 1 Cohort A (TBx or LBx analysis set)
data.populationSummary: Adult patients with advanced (locally advanced or metastatic) NSCLC with METex14 skipping alterations (TBx or LBx analysis set (all subjects tested positive for METex14 skipping alterations irrespective of testing methodology)) Summary measure: Objective Response Rate (proportion of responders).

intercurrentEvents.name: Treatment discontinuation
intercurrentEvents.text: Subject discontinues study treatment (due to AE, withdrawal of consent, progression, etc.) before achieving confirmed response or before the end of the response assessment period.

intercurrentEvents.name: Missing tumor assessment
intercurrentEvents.text: Subject has missing tumor assessment at a scheduled time point required for response evaluation.

data.treatment: Tepotinib 500 mg once daily compared to external control/natural disease progression
data.analysisPopulation: TBx or LBx analysis set (all subjects tested positive for METex14 skipping alterations irrespective of testing methodology)
data.variableOfInterest: Objective response (confirmed complete response [CR] or partial response [PR]) determined according to Response Evaluation Criteria in Solid Tumors (RECIST) Version 1.1, based on independent review
